methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
Brimstone is a Series B-stage climate and energy technology company founded by Cody Finke that has raised over $100M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 9 peers—one public company (LNZA) and eight private firms operating in the same sector and stage. The private peer set includes Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B), Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), and CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M).

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression approach, using total raised as a proxy base of $100M, applies a 1.9x multiplier typical for Series B advancement, yielding $190M. The peer-median method across six same-sector, same-stage comparables produces a median valuation of $355M, with a range spanning $120M to $5.0B. The wide spread reflects heterogeneity in the climate tech universe, particularly the outsized valuation of Commonwealth Fusion Systems.

Synthesizing these approaches yields a triangulated range of $162M (low) to $408M (high), with a median of $272M. The stage-progression method anchors the lower bound, while peer medians support the upper range. Key uncertainties include capital efficiency relative to peers, technology differentiation within the crowded climate sector, and whether Brimstone's specific technology domain commands valuation multiples closer to the peer median or toward the lower quartile of the comparable set.
